assertions.description: The schematic contains 40 SK6803Mini-E addressable RGB LEDs (D1-D40 excluding diodes). The data chain uses net SK6803_DAT_5V which connects D35 (DIN pin) and a level-shifting buffer (U2), and the component library keywords include 'RGB LED NeoPixel addressable'. Despite the chain topology and named data net, `signal_analysis.addressable_led_chains` is empty. The SK6803 part name is not recognized by the analyzer — only WS2812/WS2811-like component names appear to be matched. Contrast with Kobold keyboard where WS2812-compatible LEDs are correctly detected with chain_length=16.

The `key_matrices` detector reports a 2x2 matrix with row nets [__unnamed_0, __unnamed_73] and column nets [nBOOT, nRESET], estimating 4 keys. This is a false positive: the actual design has 43 switches (SW1-SW43 plus ENC1) with each key connected to individual k0-k47 column nets read by six MC74HC589ADTR2G parallel-in/serial-out shift registers (U4-U9). The nBOOT and nRESET signals are MCU boot/reset control lines, not keyboard matrix lines. The real key-scanning architecture (shift-register scan, not row/column matrix) was not detected, and the spurious 2x2 matrix entry may mislead analysis.
